name: Albondigas En Salsa Chipotle [Meatballs in Chipotle Sauce]
recipeInstructions:
- In a large bowl, combine all the meatball ingredients except the vegetable oil. Mix well to ensure even distribution of flavors.
- Form the mixture into about 1 1/2-inch balls and place them on a plate or in a shallow dish.
- Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Once hot, add the meatballs in batches (do not overcrowd) and brown them for about 3-4 minutes per side, until they are golden brown. Remove the meatballs from the skillet and keep warm.
For the Sauce:
1. In the same skillet, add another tablespoon of vegetable oil if needed.
2. Add chopped onions and minced garlic to the pan and sauté over medium heat for about 3-4 minutes or until softened.
3. Stir in tomato sauce, chipotles (remove stems), adobo sauce, beef broth, and bring to a simmer. Cook for 15-20 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens.
4. Puree the sauce using an immersion blender or food processor until smooth. Return the pureed sauce to the pan and return it to a gentle simmer.
Tortilla Cups:
1. Heat vegetable oil in a deep skillet or pot to about 375°F (190°C) for shallow frying.
2. Place one flour tortilla at a time into the hot oil, pressing gently with a spatula or can to form a shallow bowl shape. Fry each side until crisp and golden brown, about 1-2 minutes per side.
3. Carefully remove the tortillas from the oil using tongs and drain on paper towels.
4. To serve, place a handful of chopped lettuce into each tortilla cup, top with a single meatball, and garnish as desired.
Note: Avoid aggressive culinary terms like "kill" or "burn." Ensure all surfaces are turned off when not in use to prevent accidents.
